risks: SQL Managed Instance subnet (sql-subnet) is missing required delegation to Microsoft.Sql/managedInstances — Azure requires this for deployment
No NSG or route table defined for the SQL MI subnet — SQL MI requires both to be associated with its subnet
Load balancers (presentation-lb, app-lb) are referenced by VMSS resources but not defined in the plan
No availability zone configuration on any resource — reduces resilience against datacenter-level failures
No monitoring or diagnostics resources (e.g., Azure Monitor, Log Analytics) — limits operational visibility
No managed identity or Key Vault integration — SQL admin password uses a placeholder instead of secure secret management
App-tier VMSS has autoscale disabled with a fixed capacity of 2 — could become a bottleneck under load
correctionsRecommended: Add Standard SKU load balancer resources for presentation-lb and app-lb to resolve VMSS references
Add subnet delegation property for sql-subnet to Microsoft.Sql/managedInstances
Add NSG and route table resources associated with the SQL MI subnet
Consider enabling availability zones on VMSS and SQL MI for improved reliability
Add Azure Monitor / Log Analytics workspace for diagnostics and alerting
Integrate Azure Key Vault for SQL MI administrator credentials instead of inline placeholder
Consider enabling autoscale on the app-tier VMSS to handle variable load
Add managed identity to VMSS instances for secure access to other Azure resources
hardDependencies: Load balancers presentation-lb and app-lb are referenced by VMSS resources but absent from the resource list
SQL MI subnet delegation to Microsoft.Sql/managedInstances is not declared — required for SQL MI provisioning
